---
title: Templates
description: Templates are workspaces someone already built, iterated on, and published so others can fork them. This is the main creator path in holaOS.
---

<p className="eyebrow">For creators</p>

A **Template** is a published snapshot of a working Workspace. You don't design one from scratch in a form — you build it inside holaOS Desktop, get it genuinely working on your own tasks, then publish what you made.

Every published Template goes through the same path:

1. **Open** a blank workspace in holaOS Desktop
2. **Craft** it — install Apps, write Skills, tune the agent, run real tasks, iterate
3. **Publish** — package metadata, submit for review, ship to the marketplace

Other people can then fork your Template into a new workspace of their own, run the onboarding you wrote, and start where you left off.

## Start here

**[Build Your First Template](/docs/build/templates/first-template)** — the end-to-end walkthrough: open, craft, package, publish, and what separates a Template people actually fork from one that sits.

## What this is *not*

If you want to ship a new platform capability — a Twitter integration, a custom MCP server, a database module — you're looking for **Apps**. Apps are the building blocks Templates are made from. See the [Apps section](/docs/build/apps) instead.
